name: no_child_no_subtree_but_owns_resources
code:
proof fn phi_4_no_child_no_subtree_but_owns_resources(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c_ptr),
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().children@ =~= Seq::empty(),
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().subtree_set@ =~= Set::empty(),
{
    no_child_imply_no_subtree(root_container, container_perms, c_ptr);
}
reason: A leaf container (empty children, empty subtree) can still own threads — tree structural invariants are completely decoupled from resource ownership, so no_child_imply_no_subtree says nothing about resource cleanup
